title: Elon Musk laid out difficulty of designing, producing Optimus at scale
excerpt: Elon Musk spelled out the difficulty of making Optimus dexterous, reliable, durable, and safe, and why it will be tough to ramp up production.
content_html:
Elon Musk is discovering just how difficult it is to build a robot that can walk, talk, and think like a human.

The Tesla CEO said during the automaker's second-quarter earnings call on Wednesday that building Optimus, a humanoid robot, is a "very complex problem to solve," and "no one's ever achieved this."

While Musk said he believes Optimus will be the "biggest product ever," he flagged "many challenges" in designing it to be dexterous, reliable, durable, and safe.

For example, he said that he wants Optimus to have at least the same dexterity as a person, but the "human hand is an incredible thing."

He pointed out that "there is no supply chain" for Optimus, so Tesla has had to build one from scratch and produce some parts in-house. The automaker has to not only secure enough AI chips but also "solve memory, logic, and packaging" to make Optimus a success, he added.

Musk also underlined how tough it will be to crank out huge numbers of the robots.

"This is going to be the hardest product to scale manufacturing that we've ever made at Tesla," he said.

Musk explained that's because "everything on the robot is new," which makes it much harder to ramp up production.

The world's richest man and CEO of both Tesla and SpaceX, known for giving overly ambitious timelines, said he was managing expectations.

"I just want to make sure to calibrate people correctly," Musk said.

Tesla didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Business Insider.

Musk has previously described the magnitude of the task at hand. This time last year, he spoke about how Tesla has to design everything from the motor and gearbox to the controls and sensors, not to mention train Optimus. Yet he also said he'd be "shocked" if Tesla wasn't making 100,000 robots a month in five years' time.

Notably, Tesla described its plans for "volume" and "mass" production of Optimus in its earnings presentation last quarter, but didn't use those terms in Wednesday's presentation.

Musk stands to gain handsomely from making Optimus a success. One of the milestones he must reach to unlock the full value of his latest compensation agreement is delivering one million Optimus robots in the next decade.

blocks.html_en: While Musk said he believes Optimus will be the "biggest product ever," he flagged "many challenges" in designing it to be dexterous, reliable, durable, and safe.

blocks.html_en: For example, he said that he wants Optimus to have at least the same dexterity as a person, but the "human hand is an incredible thing."

blocks.html_en: He pointed out that "there is no supply chain" for Optimus, so Tesla has had to build one from scratch and produce some parts in-house. The automaker has to not only secure enough AI chips but also "solve memory, logic, and packaging" to make Optimus a success, he added.

sentences.en: He pointed out that "there is no supply chain" for Optimus, so Tesla has had to build one from scratch and produce some parts in-house.
sentences.zh: 他指出，擎天柱“没有供应链”，因此特斯拉不得不从头开始建造供应链，并在内部生产一些零部件。

sentences.en: The automaker has to not only secure enough AI chips but also "solve memory, logic, and packaging" to make Optimus a success, he added.
sentences.zh: 他补充说，汽车制造商不仅必须获得足够的人工智能芯片，还必须“解决内存、逻辑和封装问题”，才能使 Optimus 取得成功。

blocks.html_en: Musk also underlined how tough it will be to crank out huge numbers of the robots.

sentences.en: Musk also underlined how tough it will be to crank out huge numbers of the robots.
sentences.zh: 马斯克还强调了生产大量机器人的难度。

blocks.html_en: "This is going to be the hardest product to scale manufacturing that we've ever made at Tesla," he said.

sentences.en: "This is going to be the hardest product to scale manufacturing that we've ever made at Tesla," he said.
sentences.zh: “这将是我们特斯拉生产过的最难规模化生产的产品，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Musk explained that's because "everything on the robot is new," which makes it much harder to ramp up production.

sentences.en: Musk explained that's because "everything on the robot is new," which makes it much harder to ramp up production.
sentences.zh: 马斯克解释说，这是因为“机器人上的一切都是新的”，这使得提高产量变得更加困难。

blocks.html_en: The world's richest man and CEO of both Tesla and SpaceX, known for giving overly ambitious timelines, said he was managing expectations.

sentences.en: The world's richest man and CEO of both Tesla and SpaceX, known for giving overly ambitious timelines, said he was managing expectations.
sentences.zh: 这位世界首富兼特斯拉和 SpaceX 的首席执行官以给出过于雄心勃勃的时间表而闻名，他表示自己正在管理预期。

blocks.html_en: "I just want to make sure to calibrate people correctly," Musk said.

sentences.en: "I just want to make sure to calibrate people correctly," Musk said.
sentences.zh: “我只是想确保正确地校准人们，”马斯克说。

blocks.html_en: Tesla didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Business Insider.

sentences.zh: 特斯拉没有立即回应《商业内幕》的置评请求。

blocks.html_en: Musk has previously described the magnitude of the task at hand. This time last year, he spoke about how Tesla has to design everything from the motor and gearbox to the controls and sensors, not to mention train Optimus. Yet he also said he'd be "shocked" if Tesla wasn't making 100,000 robots a month in five years' time.

sentences.en: Musk has previously described the magnitude of the task at hand.
sentences.zh: 马斯克此前曾描述过当前任务的艰巨性。

sentences.en: This time last year, he spoke about how Tesla has to design everything from the motor and gearbox to the controls and sensors, not to mention train Optimus.
sentences.zh: 去年的这个时候，他谈到了特斯拉如何设计从电机和变速箱到控制装置和传感器的一切，更不用说擎天柱了。

sentences.en: Yet he also said he'd be "shocked" if Tesla wasn't making 100,000 robots a month in five years' time.
sentences.zh: 但他也表示，如果特斯拉在五年内没有每月生产 10 万个机器人，他会感到“震惊”。

blocks.html_en: Notably, Tesla described its plans for "volume" and "mass" production of Optimus in its earnings presentation last quarter, but didn't use those terms in Wednesday's presentation.

sentences.en: Notably, Tesla described its plans for "volume" and "mass" production of Optimus in its earnings presentation last quarter, but didn't use those terms in Wednesday's presentation.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，特斯拉在上季度的收益报告中描述了其“批量”和“批量”生产 Optimus 的计划，但在周三的报告中没有使用这些术语。

blocks.html_en: Musk stands to gain handsomely from making Optimus a success. One of the milestones he must reach to unlock the full value of his latest compensation agreement is delivering one million Optimus robots in the next decade.

sentences.en: Musk stands to gain handsomely from making Optimus a success.
sentences.zh: 马斯克将从让擎天柱取得成功中获得丰厚的收益。

sentences.en: One of the milestones he must reach to unlock the full value of his latest compensation agreement is delivering one million Optimus robots in the next decade.
sentences.zh: 为了充分发挥最新薪酬协议的全部价值，他必须实现的里程碑之一是在未来十年内交付 100 万台 Optimus 机器人。

sentences.en: Elon Musk
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克

content_html_zh:
埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 发现建造一个能够像人类一样行走、说话和思考的机器人是多么困难。

这位特斯拉首席执行官在周三举行的汽车制造商第二季度财报电话会议上表示，制造人形机器人擎天柱是一个“需要解决的非常复杂的问题”，而且“从来没有人实现过这一点”。

虽然马斯克表示，他相信 Optimus 将成为“有史以来最大的产品”，但他也指出，在将其设计得灵巧、可靠、耐用和安全方面存在“许多挑战”。

例如，他说他希望擎天柱至少拥有与人一样的灵巧性，但“人的手是一件令人难以置信的东西”。

他指出，擎天柱“没有供应链”，因此特斯拉不得不从头开始建造供应链，并在内部生产一些零部件。 他补充说，汽车制造商不仅必须获得足够的人工智能芯片，还必须“解决内存、逻辑和封装问题”，才能使 Optimus 取得成功。

马斯克还强调了生产大量机器人的难度。

“这将是我们特斯拉生产过的最难规模化生产的产品，”他说。

马斯克解释说，这是因为“机器人上的一切都是新的”，这使得提高产量变得更加困难。

这位世界首富兼特斯拉和 SpaceX 的首席执行官以给出过于雄心勃勃的时间表而闻名，他表示自己正在管理预期。

“我只是想确保正确地校准人们，”马斯克说。

特斯拉没有立即回应《商业内幕》的置评请求。

马斯克此前曾描述过当前任务的艰巨性。 去年的这个时候，他谈到了特斯拉如何设计从电机和变速箱到控制装置和传感器的一切，更不用说擎天柱了。 但他也表示，如果特斯拉在五年内没有每月生产 10 万个机器人，他会感到“震惊”。

值得注意的是，特斯拉在上季度的收益报告中描述了其“批量”和“批量”生产 Optimus 的计划，但在周三的报告中没有使用这些术语。

马斯克将从让擎天柱取得成功中获得丰厚的收益。 为了充分发挥最新薪酬协议的全部价值，他必须实现的里程碑之一是在未来十年内交付 100 万台 Optimus 机器人。
